expectations.text: A handoff markdown file was created in a thoughts/handoffs/ or thoughts/shared/handoffs/ directory
expectations.evidence: File created at thoughts/shared/handoffs/general/ via manual path construction

expectations.text: The handoff file has valid YAML frontmatter containing at least date and branch fields
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter has 12 fields including date, branch, researcher, topic, tags, status, etc.

expectations.text: The handoff document is under 200 lines total
The Tasks section describes the debugging investigation and its status (root cause found, fix not implemented)
expectations.evidence: Lines 17-26: 'Completed: Root cause analysis' with detailed status

expectations.text: The Learnings section captures the deadlock root cause: that the previous mutex fix was reverted because Dispatch() RLock conflicts with handler Lock
expectations.evidence: Lines 42-47: detailed deadlock explanation with commit references

expectations.text: The document references thoughts/debug-notes.md as prior investigation notes
expectations.evidence: Line 32: 'thoughts/debug-notes.md - Detailed findings and fix options'

expectations.text: The document mentions the three fix options (sync.Map, channel, restructure locking)
expectations.evidence: Lines 57-59: all three options listed with descriptions

expectations.text: Action items specify implementing one of the fix approaches, not just 'continue debugging'
expectations.evidence: Lines 56-68: detailed action items with preference ordering and validation steps

expectations.text: The document does not contain code blocks longer than 10 lines
The response to the user includes the /resume-handoff command with the handoff file path
expectations.evidence: Agent responded with '/resume-handoff' and file path

suggestions.reason: Old skill output self-references in Artifacts: 'This handoff document' (line 52) — circular reference adds no value. Also has 'Recent changes' section listing just one line of debug code added, which is trivially discoverable via git diff.

eval_feedback.overall: Passes all assertions. Learnings section is actually quite good — detailed deadlock explanation. But 75 lines vs 40 for new skill, with noise in Artifacts and Recent changes.
